option.name: originalContent
option.value: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <module type="JAVA_MODULE" version="4"> <component name="NewModuleRootManager" inherit-compiler-output="true"> <exclude-output /> <content url="file://$MODULE_DIR$"> <sourceFolder url="file://$MODULE_DIR$/src" isTestSource="false" /> </content> <orderEntry type="inheritedJdk" /> <orderEntry type="sourceFolder" forTests="false" /> </component> </module>

option.name: updatedContent
option.value: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <module type="JAVA_MODULE" version="4"> <component name="NewModuleRootManager" inherit-compiler-output="true"> <exclude-output /> <content url="file://$MODULE_DIR$"> <sourceFolder url="file://$MODULE_DIR$/src" isTestSource="false" /> </content> <orderEntry type="module-library"> <library> <CLASSES> <root url="jar://$MODULE_DIR$/lib/gson-2.14.0.jar!/" /> </CLASSES> <JAVADOC /> <SOURCES /> </library> </orderEntry> <orderEntry type="inheritedJdk" /> <orderEntry type="sourceFolder" forTests="false" /> </component> </module>

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# JavaProyectSocket Calculadora distribuida cliente-servidor usando sockets TCP y un protocolo de texto plano. La logica de operaciones se registra con reflexion en el servidor.
## Requisitos - JDK instalado y `java`/`javac` disponibles en el PATH. - Libreria Gson en `lib/gson-2.14.0.jar`.
## Compilar javac -d out src\server\*.java src\client\*.java javac -cp "lib\gson-2.14.0.jar" -d out src\common\*.java src\server\*.java src\client\*.java ```
## Ejecutar Servidor: ```powershell java -cp out server.ServerMain ```powershell java -cp "out;lib\gson-2.14.0.jar" common.JsonSmokeTest ```
## Protocolo (JSON) Peticion: ```json {"operacion":"SUMA","datos":[10,5]} ```
Respuesta OK: ```json {"estado":"OK","resultado":15.0} java -cp out client.ClientMain
Respuesta ERROR: ```json ## Smoke test (sin sockets) ```powershell ```
